rxn_class_summary: Ferrocene-mediated electrochemical dehydrogenative (3+2) annulation of 1,1-disubstituted alkenes with 1,3-diesters (dimethyl 2-benzylmalonates) to give gamma-butyrolactones
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of ferrocene (Cp2Fe) to Cp2Fe+ at the carbon felt anode acts as a redox mediator that oxidizes the malonate carbanion to a C-centered radical; cathodic reduction of H2O at the Pt cathode generates OH- (used as base) and H2

key_intermediates.name: Malonate carbon anion (from deprotonation of benzyl dimethyl malonate by OH-)
Radical A: malonyl C-radical (alpha-carbon radical of benzyl dimethyl malonate after SET oxidation by Cp2Fe+)
Radical B: benzylic radical from addition of A to 1,1-diphenylethylene
Carbocation C: benzylic cation after second SET oxidation of B by Cp2Fe+
Benzyl alcohol intermediate D (after OH- addition to C)
gamma-Butyrolactone product (after intramolecular lactonization with loss of MeOH)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes Cp2Fe to Cp2Fe+ while cathode reduces H2O to OH- + H2; OH- deprotonates the 1,3-diester and Cp2Fe+ performs SET on the resulting carbanion to give a malonyl radical, which adds to the 1,1-diarylalkene to form a benzylic radical; a second SET by Cp2Fe+ generates a benzylic cation that is trapped by hydroxide, and the resulting alcohol lactonizes onto an ester (loss of MeOH) to give the gamma-butyrolactone.

probe_reagents.name: H2 18-O (isotopically labeled water)
probe_reagents.observation: 18O-labeled product 1' isolated in 22% yield (86% 18O incorporation) using MeOH solvent with H2(18O) additive, showing the lactone oxygen originates from water, not from substrate or methanol

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, 2a, and Cp2Fe
probe_reagents.observation: No oxidation of 1a or 2a in 0-1.0 V vs Ag/AgCl; Cp2Fe shows reversible wave at ~0.45 V; catalytic current of Cp2Fe increases with added 1a (with base), consistent with electron transfer from the malonate anion to Cp2Fe+

probe_reagents.name: Controlled-potential electrolysis
probe_reagents.observation: At 0.3 V vs Ag/AgCl (below Cp2Fe oxidation) only 9% product after 20 h; at 0.6 V (above Cp2Fe oxidation) 80% product in 1.67 h, confirming Cp2Fe-mediated pathway

notes: X-ray structure (CCDC 2382826) of product 7 confirms gamma-butyrolactone connectivity. The mediator avoids direct anodic oxidation of the malonate, which is proposed to cause overoxidation; mono-substituted electron-rich alkenes give difunctionalized methoxy products instead of lactones (alternative trapping by MeOH on the cation), and benzyl acrylate gives a (4+2) product, indicating the radical-polar crossover step depends on alkene electronics.
